tasks.title: Persistent RunRepository adapter shipped and verified
tasks.description: Completed Stage 1 / Epic 1 run persistence by replacing bootstrap's default `InMemoryRunRepository` path with a SQLAlchemy-backed durable adapter, persisting full `RunRecord` payloads to `.local/state/runs.sqlite3` by default, exposing `AKRA_TRADER_RUNS_DATABASE_URL`, and adding restart-persistence coverage in the API and application tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the durable run-storage implementation, verified it with `pytest` (`13 passed`), and committed the change as `d6e3041`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly targets research-foundation success criteria for restart-safe runs and reproducible experiment records.

tasks.title: RunRepository deployment database wiring shipped
tasks.description: Completed the deployed-environment RunRepository follow-up by adding the Postgres driver dependency, enabling SQLAlchemy `pool_pre_ping`, pointing Compose `AKRA_TRADER_RUNS_DATABASE_URL` at the `postgres` service with health-gated startup, adding bootstrap coverage for a configured non-SQLite URL, and documenting the Compose/Postgres behavior.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the Postgres deployment wiring, verified API tests (`15 passed`), and committed the change as `aa2b6f4`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closes the deployment-database extension of the research-foundation persistence work without reopening the completed adapter implementation.

tasks.title: Smoke test Compose startup against Postgres-backed run storage
tasks.description: Run an end-to-end Docker Compose startup on a machine with Docker installed to confirm the API waits for Postgres health, boots successfully with `AKRA_TRADER_RUNS_DATABASE_URL` pointed at the `postgres` service, and persists run storage without SQLite fallback.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply called this the only unresolved follow-up from the deployment-database work, but execution still needs a Docker-capable environment outside the current workspace.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Provides the remaining deployment validation for research-foundation persistence without adding new product scope.

tasks.title: Binance-backed candle ingestion and sync status shipped
tasks.description: Completed the Track A / Epic 2 market-data foundation by replacing the default seeded path with a SQL-backed Binance ingestion adapter, persisting OHLCV coverage with freshness reuse, surfacing `sync_status` / lag / gap signals through the API and web UI, and keeping `seeded` only as an offline test fallback.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the Binance-backed ingestion implementation, verified API and web coverage, and committed the change as `74c3a39`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly targets the research-foundation success criterion that market data be Binance-backed with lag visibility and quality checks.

tasks.title: Binance market-data sync now runs as a scheduled background job
tasks.description: Completed the request-path sync removal by moving Binance refresh into a scheduled `MarketDataSyncJob`, wiring startup/shutdown through the FastAPI lifespan, adding sync interval/timeframe configuration, and covering both unsynced request behavior and job lifecycle with API tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the scheduled Binance sync migration, verified it with `pytest` (`27 passed`), and committed the change as `f722373`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the research-foundation market-data lane from request-time freshness checks to predictable ingestion operations suitable for real trading workflows.

tasks.title: Binance historical backfill beyond rolling sync windows shipped
tasks.description: Completed the historical coverage follow-up by teaching Binance background sync to keep the recent window current and then walk backward in batches until a configurable historical candle target is reached, wiring `AKRA_TRADER_MARKET_DATA_HISTORICAL_CANDLE_LIMIT` through bootstrap/config, and adding API/bootstrap coverage for expanding stored history beyond the rolling sync window.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the historical Binance backfill implementation, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`), and committed the change as `b64edf2`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the research-foundation ingestion lane from short-horizon freshness into reproducible historical coverage needed for credible research and replay.

tasks.title: Binance historical backfill target and completion now exposed in ops status
tasks.description: Completed the operator-facing backfill status follow-up by extending the API `InstrumentStatus` shape with historical target, completion ratio, and completion flag fields, populating them for Binance-backed symbols while keeping seeded payloads compatible, and surfacing both summary tiles and per-instrument progress bars in the control-room market-data table.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the backfill visibility work, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `69893ae`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the control-room goal that operators can tell whether Binance data is only recently refreshed or fully backfilled to the configured historical target.

tasks.title: Binance ops now separates backfill count completion from contiguous coverage quality
tasks.description: Completed the ops quality follow-up by splitting Binance instrument status into distinct count-completion and contiguous-quality views, exposing missing-candle-aware quality fields through the API model, surfacing separate control-room summary tiles plus per-instrument Count and Quality columns, and locking in the 'count complete but gap exists' case with API and web verification.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the count-versus-quality split in ops status, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `4da9d13`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Pushes the control-room ops surface from simple backfill progress toward operator-trustworthy data quality signals about whether historical coverage is actually continuous enough to rely on.

tasks.title: Exact Binance gap windows are now shown with backfill quality status
tasks.description: Completed the gap-window diagnostics follow-up by deriving concrete missing candle ranges from Binance candle timestamps, exposing them as `backfill_gap_windows` in instrument status, and rendering precise `start -> end (missing_count)` ranges in the control-room UI with API and web coverage for gap-free and single-gap cases.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the exact gap-window surface, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `589bbdc`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the control-room trust surface from coarse quality alarms toward operator-actionable diagnostics by showing where historical Binance coverage actually breaks.

tasks.title: Fragmented Binance gap windows now collapse into a readable ops summary
tasks.description: Completed the fragmented-gap follow-up by collapsing long Binance gap-window lists in the control-room quality cell, preserving a few recent exact windows while rolling older fragments into a compact summary line with range and missing-count context, plus matching UI treatment for the collapsed state.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the fragmented gap-window rollup, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `e03deb3`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps the control-room gap diagnostics operator-usable as Binance history grows more fragmented, so the new quality surface stays readable instead of turning into raw noise.

tasks.title: On-demand expand/collapse control for rolled-up Binance gap windows shipped
tasks.description: Completed the rolled-gap follow-up by adding an inline `Show all N gaps` / collapse control to the control-room quality cell so rolled-up Binance gap windows stay collapsed by default but can be expanded on demand to inspect the full exact list, with matching inline control styling in the web UI.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the expand/collapse control, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `85ffbdb`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the rolled-up gap diagnostics so the control room stays readable by default while preserving access to exact Binance missing-window detail when operators need to investigate.

tasks.title: Expanded Binance gap-window rows now persist across refreshes
tasks.description: Completed the refresh-persistence follow-up by lifting rolled-up Binance gap-window expand/collapse state to app scope, keying it by instrument and timeframe, persisting it in `localStorage`, and verifying the web app with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` before committing `8e4e4ca`.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the refresh-safe expand/collapse persistence work, verified it in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `8e4e4ca` (`feat(ops): persist expanded gap rows`).
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the control-room gap diagnostics so operator investigation survives periodic UI refreshes instead of collapsing long Binance gap lists mid-review.

tasks.title: Stale persisted Binance gap-window expansion keys are pruned
tasks.description: Completed the stale-key cleanup follow-up by filtering persisted Binance gap-window expand/collapse state against the current ops payload so removed or no-longer-rolled-up rows are dropped from `localStorage` during market-data status refreshes instead of accumulating obsolete keys.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the stale-key pruning work in `apps/web`, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build`, and committed the change as `a53a1a9` (`feat(ops): prune stale gap row state`).
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps the control-room gap-window persistence trustworthy over time by ensuring operator-facing refresh state stays aligned with the currently tracked Binance rows.

tasks.title: Persisted control-room UI state is now versioned before storage scope grows
tasks.description: Completed the control-room persistence follow-up by wrapping stored UI state in a versioned `control-room-ui-state` envelope, preserving fallback reads from the legacy `akra-trader-gap-window-expansion` key, and writing only the versioned payload while removing the old key.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the control-room UI state versioning work, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `b6fa551` (`feat(ops): version control room ui state`).
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps control-room persistence maintainable as more operator-facing UI preferences become durable, so future state additions do not strand incompatible browser payloads.

tasks.title: Add explicit migration handling when a second persisted control-room preference lands
tasks.description: Once another control-room UI preference is introduced into the shared storage envelope, add an explicit version-to-version migration path and coverage so stored operator preferences upgrade intentionally instead of relying only on the current legacy fallback from the pre-envelope gap-row map.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply said the remaining follow-up is explicit migration handling for a future second UI preference, but that work should stay a proposal until the broader persisted preference scope actually exists.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps the control-room persistence lane forward-compatible by reserving real migration work for the point where multiple durable operator preferences share one versioned browser payload.

tasks.title: Broaden Binance symbol and timeframe prewarm configuration
tasks.description: Expand configuration beyond the default tracked set so operators can declare additional Binance symbols, timeframes, and startup prewarm coverage targets, with validation and tests that keep ingestion scope explicit.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply named broader symbol/timeframe configuration as a secondary follow-up, but scheduled syncing is the more immediate queue-head task.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Supports the same research-foundation ingestion lane while keeping configurable market coverage separate from the scheduling implementation.

tasks.title: Control-room run lineage panel shipped
tasks.description: Completed the control-room lineage follow-up by surfacing `run.provenance.market_data` inside each run card, including provider, sync status, candle count, timeframe, venue and symbols, requested vs effective windows, last sync time, and lineage issues, with dedicated UI styling for provenance detail.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the control-room lineage UI, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `73edd55`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly advances the control-room goal that operator surfaces explain what data a run used, how complete it was, and whether it is safe to trust.

tasks.title: Per-symbol market-data lineage capture shipped for multi-symbol runs
tasks.description: Completed the provenance follow-up by extending run lineage beyond one aggregate `market_data` record so native and delegated multi-symbol runs now persist symbol-scoped lineage in `market_data_by_symbol` while keeping the aggregate field for compatibility, with API/application/freqtrade coverage and serialization tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the per-symbol lineage implementation, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`24 passed`), and committed the change as `0d8d44b`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds directly on the research-foundation reproducibility work by making dataset provenance precise enough for multi-symbol comparisons and audits.

tasks.title: Per-symbol lineage now surfaced in control-room run details
tasks.description: Completed the control-room follow-up by rendering `provenance.market_data_by_symbol` in run details so multi-symbol runs now expose symbol-scoped sync status, candle counts, effective windows, last sync time, and lineage issues alongside the aggregate lineage block, while older runs gracefully fall back to aggregate-only display.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the per-symbol lineage control-room surface, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `a71d3a9`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the control-room goal that operators can inspect and trust multi-symbol run provenance from one surface, with backward-compatible fallback for older runs that only retain aggregate lineage.

tasks.title: Strategy lifecycle metadata and version filters shipped across catalog and runs
tasks.description: Completed the Epic 3 metadata follow-up by extending `StrategyMetadata` with lifecycle and version-lineage fields, carrying lineage into stored strategy snapshots, exposing lane/lifecycle/version filtering through the strategies API, adding run-history filtering by `strategy_id` and `strategy_version`, and surfacing lifecycle, version lineage, and parameter snapshots in the control-room strategy and run views.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the shared strategy metadata and run-filtering work and verified it with `pytest` (`32 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Covers the strategy-platform success criteria around explicit runtime lane metadata and comparable version lifecycle.

tasks.title: Run comparison workflow for native and reference benchmarks shipped
tasks.description: Completed the Stage 2 compare-runs workflow by adding ordered multi-run lookup plus `GET /api/runs/compare`, returning side-by-side comparison payloads for total return, max drawdown, win rate, trade count, run notes, lane/status metadata, and strategy/version context, then wiring the control room to select up to four runs, auto-pick a native versus NFI baseline pair, and render baseline deltas with lane visibility.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the compare-runs workflow end to end, verified it with `.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`34 passed`), `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run build`, and pushed commit `d36c99b` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly advances the research-foundation success criteria around reproducible experiments and straightforward comparison across strategy versions and benchmark lanes.

tasks.title: Persist reference-run provenance and artifact links for benchmark comparisons
tasks.description: Completed the benchmark-comparison provenance follow-up by persisting reference catalog snapshots into reference-lane run provenance, preserving benchmark-specific provenance and deterministic artifact paths in comparison payloads, teaching the Freqtrade adapter to retain expected result/log locations even when `freqtrade` is unavailable, and exposing the new provenance across control-room run and comparison views.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the reference-run provenance and artifact-link follow-up, verified it with `.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`34 passed`), `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run build`, and pushed commit `fe96a26` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Supports the reference-lanes success criteria that reference runs stay explicit and traceable rather than becoming hidden native equivalents.

tasks.title: Typed benchmark artifacts now back reference-lane provenance
tasks.description: Completed the typed benchmark artifact follow-up by adding a `BenchmarkArtifact` model to reference-run provenance and comparison payloads, teaching the Freqtrade adapter to classify deterministic artifact paths into typed benchmark artifacts while preserving backward-compatible flat `artifact_paths`, surfacing the richer artifact metadata in normal and comparison payloads plus the control room, and adding backend assertions for typed artifact kinds.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the typed benchmark artifact implementation, verified `apps/api` with `.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`34 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `f2a9354` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference-lanes benchmark surface from generic artifact links toward operator-meaningful benchmark evidence without collapsing external-runtime runs into native semantics.

tasks.title: Typed benchmark artifacts now ingest Freqtrade manifests and summaries
tasks.description: Completed the manifest-enrichment follow-up by extending `BenchmarkArtifact` with structured summary metadata and `summary_source_path`, teaching the Freqtrade reference adapter to enrich typed artifacts from `.meta.json` manifests, result `.json` summaries, sibling manifests for `.zip` snapshots, and backtest result roots, then surfacing the normalized summary fields through stored provenance, comparison payloads, and control-room artifact rows with synthetic parsing coverage.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the manifest and summary enrichment work, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`36 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `ecc1a78` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps reference-lane benchmark evidence explicit and operator-meaningful by grounding typed artifacts in real Freqtrade outputs instead of convention-only path heuristics.

tasks.title: Richer nested Freqtrade benchmark sections are now parsed into artifact summaries
tasks.description: Completed the nested-summary follow-up by reworking the Freqtrade artifact parser around the actual export layout (`metadata[strategy_name]`, `strategy[strategy_name]`, `strategy_comparison`, and nested strategy sections), adding structured `sections` to `BenchmarkArtifact`, improving ratio normalization for UI-ready percentages, surfacing nested benchmark sections in the control room, and replacing synthetic tests with fixtures mirroring real nested Freqtrade JSON.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the nested Freqtrade summary parsing work, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`36 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `9b4e75f` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference benchmark lane from normalized artifact summaries toward benchmark-native semantics by capturing the nested Freqtrade structures that operators compare directly.

tasks.title: Inspect Freqtrade zip artifacts directly for benchmark provenance
tasks.description: Completed the direct zip-inspection follow-up by teaching the Freqtrade reference adapter to open benchmark `.zip` artifacts directly, extract provenance from embedded result/config JSON plus strategy bundles and source files, record export presence for wallet/market-change/signals/rejected/exited artifacts, surface the new `zip_contents`, `zip_config`, and `zip_strategy_bundle` sections through benchmark artifact payloads and control-room labels, and cover both direct zip inspection and root-level provenance fallback with adapter tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the direct Freqtrade zip-artifact inspection work, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`37 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `9347fbf` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference benchmark lane from parsed result summaries toward fuller benchmark provenance by reading the packaged Freqtrade evidence operators actually export and inspect.

tasks.title: Embedded Freqtrade feather and pickle exports now contribute structural provenance
tasks.description: Completed the binary-export inspection follow-up by teaching the Freqtrade reference adapter to read embedded `*_market_change.feather`, `*_wallet.feather`, `*_signals.pkl`, `*_rejected.pkl`, and `*_exited.pkl` members inside benchmark zip snapshots, extract structural provenance such as row/frame counts, column lists, time ranges, strategy/pair counts, wallet currency summaries, and preview entries, keep parsing defensive when binary members are unreadable, add the required `joblib` and `pyarrow` runtime dependencies, and surface the new binary-export sections through control-room labels.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the embedded Freqtrade binary-export inspection, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`37 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `0322740` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the reference benchmark lane from zip-level inventory into deeper benchmark-native provenance by reading the packaged binary exports that still remain opaque to operators and comparison views.

tasks.title: Decode richer semantics from embedded Freqtrade binary exports
tasks.description: Completed the semantic-decoding follow-up by extending the Freqtrade zip inspector to read richer meaning from embedded binary exports, including market-change best/worst pairs and positive/negative counts, wallet portfolio start/end/high/low quote values plus per-currency latest quote previews, and signal/rejected/exited previews with `enter_tag`, reject-reason, and exit-reason breakdown counts, while keeping unreadable members non-fatal and covering the new behavior with real in-memory feather/joblib payload tests plus control-room labels.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the richer Freqtrade binary-export semantic decoding, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`37 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `117bc55` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference benchmark lane from structural binary-export provenance toward richer benchmark-native semantics that operators can compare directly without opening raw Freqtrade artifacts.

tasks.title: Higher-level benchmark narratives from decoded Freqtrade exports shipped
tasks.description: Completed the benchmark narrative follow-up by synthesizing decoded Freqtrade zip exports into artifact/root `benchmark_story` sections that summarize run headline, market breadth, wallet trajectory, signal bias, rejection friction, exit posture, and pair concentration, then surfacing those readable labels in the web artifact cards and locking both snapshot/root narrative outputs into API tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the higher-level Freqtrade benchmark narrative synthesis, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`37 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `07b6caf` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Pushes the reference benchmark lane past raw decoded sections into operator-usable benchmark narratives that improve quick comparison without collapsing external-runtime evidence into native analytics.

tasks.title: Explain native-vs-reference benchmark divergence with comparison-level narratives
tasks.description: Completed the comparison-narrative follow-up by extending native-vs-reference run comparison payloads with structured comparison-level `narratives`, teaching the application layer to classify native/reference pairings and synthesize divergence headlines, summaries, and supporting bullets with fallback to reference benchmark artifact summaries, then surfacing the resulting narrative cards above the metric table in the control room with API and application regression coverage.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the comparison-level divergence narratives, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`38 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `9fb1958` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference benchmark lane from isolated benchmark storytelling into direct native-versus-reference interpretation so operators can understand divergence without manually reconciling separate summaries.

tasks.title: Rank multi-run divergence narratives so comparisons surface the top insight first
tasks.description: Completed the multi-run ranking follow-up by scoring comparison-level divergence narratives with `insight_score`, annotating each narrative with `rank` and `is_primary`, ordering multi-run insights by return, drawdown, win-rate, trade-flow, and native-vs-reference context, and surfacing the top-ranked narrative first in the control room with regression coverage for both application ordering and API payload fields.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the multi-run divergence narrative ranking, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`39 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `6ea5716` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the reference benchmark comparison surface from per-run divergence explanations into operator-prioritized storytelling for multi-run benchmark sessions where the most important divergence should appear first.

tasks.title: Make divergence narrative ranking regime-aware by comparison intent
tasks.description: Refine the newly shipped `insight_score` ordering so benchmark validation, execution regression, and strategy-tuning comparisons can weight drawdown, trade-flow, and related signals differently, with explicit comparison-intent heuristics or inputs, deterministic ranking behavior, and regression coverage that proves the top insight changes appropriately by regime.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply said nothing essential remains for the completed ranking work and explicitly called out regime-aware weighting as the likely next refinement, making this the clearest executable follow-up for the benchmark comparison queue.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the reference benchmark comparison surface from one-size-fits-all insight ordering into intent-aware prioritization so operators see the most relevant divergence first for the kind of comparison they are running.
